answer:
\boxed{D}

An **ALU (Arithmetic Logic Unit)** can be constructed using only NAND gates because NAND gates are universal gates—they can implement all other logic gates (AND, OR, NOT) through combinations. While a full CPU, RAM, or register requires additional components (e.g., memory cells, clock circuits), an ALU’s basic logic functions (addition, comparison, etc.) rely solely on combinational logic, which NAND gates can fully realize.
